title: allium maritimum
intro: Allium maritimum, also known as sea garlic, is a perennial plant that belongs to the onion family and is native to the Mediterranean coastal regions. The medicinal properties of sea garlic lie in its high allicin content, a compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Allium maritimum include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Sea garlic can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
